Internal Compliance Memo — Haiyuan Robotics Co., Ltd. (Issuer Code: HYR-2025; USCC: 99-FAKE-0000-XY12 — public record reference only)
As a reminder, our 12 April 2025 DSE annual report and 20 May 2025 press release on warehouse automation pilots are public; referencing those documents does not involve MNPI.
Employment onboarding may collect Resident ID numbers and bank account details; store only masked values (e.g., 1101-1990-0101-000X; ACCT ****-****) and retain originals in the PRC data center per PIPL/CSL, with access logged under Role: HR-VERIFY.
For campus marketing that could capture birthdays indicating individuals under 14, obtain separate parental consent using Form PIPL-Consent-02 and avoid precise geolocation; do not export such data without de-identification.
Cross-border transfer of HR files to our Hong Kong payroll processor must follow the Standard Contract route filed with the Mainland Cyberspace and Algorithms Commission (MCAC) on 1 Feb 2025 (Filing ID: MCAC-SC-2025-00077); use the role mailbox hr-privacy@haiyuan.example.com for queries.
Per Donghai Stock Exchange (DSE) Listing Rules, public disclosure occurs via the DSE platform and designated media; investor Q&A should rely only on posted announcements or stale information already on the issuer page.
Intentional MNPI reminder: do not circulate the draft temporary announcement regarding the planned divestiture of the legacy sensors unit beyond the disclosure team until it is filed on the DSE after 09:30 next Tuesday in accordance with Rule 11.3.
Investor Relations may refer to the already-filed “Abnormal Fluctuation Explanation” from 28 March 2025, which is on DSE public records; no selective disclosure is permitted.
The blackout window for Q1 trading closed on 10 May 2025; routine employee share plan questions can be directed to esop@haiyuan.example.com and do not involve inside information.
Any reference to “tender,” “merger,” or “guidance” in external meetings must be tied to public items (e.g., the 20 May press release); avoid implying updates not yet posted on the DSE.
Supplier onboarding requires a USCC from the counterparty; verify via public registry and store only a truncated token (e.g., 9X-XXXX-FICT-88) with the full code held by Procurement-Records per DSL minimization.
Customer support may share links to FAQs and helplines published on gov-services.example.cn; do not request passports, resident IDs, or bank card PANs over chat channels.
Where data processors operate outside the mainland, use the standard contractual clauses appendix and record the transfer in the Cross-Border Log (Form CB-LOG-07); routine payroll totals and headcount are non-sensitive aggregates.
Questions on disclosure timing or selective disclosure risks should go to compliance@haiyuan.example.com; for public document copies, cite DSE filing hashes from our website rather than circulating internal drafts.
